methods.description: Application of the LOVECLIM Earth model of intermediate complexity to simulate modern and past climates. The model includes coupled atmosphere, ocean, sea-ice, and vegetation components to study climate dynamics and sensitivity.

steps.description: We applied LOVECLIM, an Earth model of intermediate complexity, which is comprised of a coupled atmosphere, ocean, sea-ice and vegetation model.
steps.searchable_summary: apply LOVECLIM Earth model for climate simulation

steps.description: The atmospheric component (ECBilt) is a quasi-geostrophic model of the atmosphere with a horizontal T21 truncation and three vertical layers, 800, 500 and 200 hPa.
steps.searchable_summary: use ECBilt atmospheric model with T21 truncation

steps.description: Clouds are prescribed based on the ISCCP D2 data set, with the total upward and downward radiative fluxes computed as a function of this data set.
steps.searchable_summary: compute radiative fluxes using ISCCP D2 data

steps.description: The oceanic component (CLIO) is a primitive-equation, free-surface general ocean circulation model coupled to a thermodynamic-dynamic sea-ice model.
The vegetation component (VECODE) is a reduced-form dynamic global vegetation model, simulating dynamics of trees, grasses, and desert.
LOVECLIM simulates a modern climate that is in reasonable agreement with observations, including large-scale distributions of temperature and precipitation, and sea-ice cover.
